title: plantago maritima subsp. serpentina
intro: Plantago maritima subsp. serpentina, also known as Sea Plantain, is a subspecies of the Plantago maritima plant that is native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The medicinal properties of Plantago maritima subsp. serpentina are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which can provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. Plantago maritima subsp. serpentina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
